
arXiv signals yielded two candidates meeting ≥3 criteria. Grok social signals have fabricated tweet IDs and are deprioritized. Top pick is 2604.15877 on unified memory/skills/rules architecture for LLM agents; second is 2604.15972 on multi-agent weak-link optimization with empirical grounding.

## Suggestions

- [Experience Compression Spectrum: Unifying Memory, Skills, and Rules in LLM Agents](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.15877) — Proposes a unified framework spanning memory, skills, and rules as complementary experience compression mechanisms in LLM agents, with architectural implications for state management, tool-use design, and iterative refinement across the agent lifecycle.
  Section: Research Papers | Criteria: Architectural guidance for goal-directed agent systems (patterns, orchestration, state management), Tool-use and execution design across agent lifecycle stages (planning, execution, feedback, recovery), Iterative refinement practices (evaluation loops, human-in-the-loop, observability)

- [Weak-Link Optimization for Multi-Agent Reasoning and Collaboration](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.15972) — Introduces a weak-link optimization principle for identifying and strengthening bottleneck agents in multi-agent reasoning pipelines, with empirical evaluation on collaborative task performance.
  Section: Research Papers | Criteria: Architectural guidance for goal-directed agent systems (patterns, orchestration, state management), Tool-use and execution design across agent lifecycle stages (planning, execution, feedback, recovery), Empirical or practical grounding (case studies, benchmarks, production deployment evidence)
